name: jq (Command-Line JSON Processor)

performance.notes: Native C implementation; extremely fast even for large JSON

agent_readiness.notes: The standard JSON processor for CLI/shell workflows. `jq '.' file.json` pretty-print. `.field`, `.field.nested`, `.array[]`, `.[0]` selectors. map(), select(), has(), keys(), values(), to_entries, from_entries. String interpolation '\(.field)'. Pipe `|` chaining. reduce, foreach, until. --arg, --argjson for variables. --raw-output (-r) for strings. Streaming large JSON. Available on all platforms (brew, apt, winget). Used in CI/CD, APIs, data processing. yq for YAML equivalent.

gotchas: Not a library/SDK — CLI tool only
Error messages can be cryptic for advanced filters
Not suitable for very large files (streaming needed)
best_when: Shell scripts and CI/CD pipelines processing JSON
Extracting values from API responses in bash/zsh
avoid_when: Programmatic JSON in Python/JS code (use native json/JSON)
Complex data transformations better done in Python/jq alternatives
summary: jq is the standard CLI JSON processor. Filter, transform, and query JSON from shell scripts: `curl api | jq '.data[].name'`. Field access, array operations, map/select/reduce, pipes. Available everywhere. Ubiquitous in CI/CD pipelines, API testing, and data processing scripts. Best for shell-based JSON manipulation. Use yq for YAML, Python json module for programmatic processing.
